title: orchis morio subsp. albanica
intro: Orchis morio subsp. albanica, also known as the Albanian green orchid or green orchid, is a subspecies of the green orchid (Orchis morio) found in the Balkans and Albania. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal purposes.
